title: Cross-Section of the Arm and its Compartments
structures: triceps brachii (long head, medial head, lateral head)
description: Transverse cross-section of the mid-arm illustrating the anterior and posterior compartments separated by the medial and lateral intermuscular septa. The diagram highlights the central humerus and the anatomical relationships of major nerves (median, ulnar, radial, musculocutaneous) and vessels (brachial, basilic, cephalic) to the surrounding musculature.
clinical_notes: The radial nerve is vulnerable to mid-shaft humeral fractures due to its proximity to the bone and lateral septum. Injury results in radial nerve palsy (wrist drop). OT role: fabrication of dynamic extension orthoses to substitute for lost motor function, prevention of flexion contractures, and functional task adaptation during nerve regeneration.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 11 – The Muscular System & Chapter 13 – The Peripheral Nervous System
